diff --git a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex b/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
index 51ab643757e0de19b750d9e7294946d79ab73721..67391deb00c254dbbdd4ffe918d580548fd0c821
--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}

-$\nabla = d + k\,d\log w$ with monodromy $e^{2\pi i k}$;
+The degree-$2$ flat connection is
+$\nabla = d + k\,dw/w$ with monodromy $e^{2\pi i k}$;
 for generic $k \notin \bZ$, the twisted de~Rham cohomology
 of~$\bC^\times$ vanishes and $\mathrm{HH}^2$ is
 one-dimensional. Total dimension: $1 + 1 + 1 = 3$.

--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

 The two forms are distinct: $\eta_{ij}$ is the logarithmic
-connection one-form, weight~$1$ under conformal rescaling, which
-lives in $H^1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\bC))$; the propagator
+Arnold one-form, weight~$1$ under conformal rescaling, which
+lives in $H^1(\mathrm{Conf}_2(\bC))$ and serves as the
+bar-complex coefficient; the propagator
 $k\,dz_i/(z_i - z_j)^2$ is weight~$2$ and is the actual Feynman
 propagator of the perturbative expansion. They are related by
 the identity $dz_i/(z_i - z_j)^2 = -\partial_{z_i}(1/(z_i - z_j))$,

@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex

@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

**Organizing file issues**

I’m considering the best way to address multiple lines in a file while focusing on the user’s request for a singular line. It might help to compress the information by file cluster, potentially summarizing as "PROBLEM at [file]:444, 2230, 3585, 5109, 6552." I want it to be concise yet thorough—likely around 30-40 lines total from about 10 writable files and possibly 12-13 blocked read-only files. I'll gather exact line numbers using specific commands to streamline this process.

index 51ab643757e0de19b750d9e7294946d79ab73721..67391deb00c254dbbdd4ffe918d580548fd0c821
--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

The Arnold relations are precisely the conditions ensuring this equation holds.
 
 \subsubsection{Concrete computation}

**Finding line numbers**

I need to gather exact line numbers for our files. I think using `rg` would be simpler with multiple patterns, but I could also use `nl -ba` around the changed sections. The `rg -n` command can help with specific phrases, too. For final output, I might reference line numbers from a diff. I'll aim for stable current line numbers and generate a concise list, possibly using `rg -n` for `dz/z` and others per file.

diff --git a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex b/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
index 51ab643757e0de19b750d9e7294946d79ab73721..67391deb00c254dbbdd4ffe918d580548fd0c821
--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
+++ b/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex
@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

 on $\mathrm{Conf}_n^{\mathrm{ord}}(\mathbb{C})$.

 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex

@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

--- a/chapters/theory/fourier_seed.tex

@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

thinking
**Considering file link formats**

I need to think about whether to use absolute or relative paths for file links. Final file links should probably be absolute, but I could also consider local file links. The user specified a format of "PROBLEM at file:line," which might suggest simple text instead. However, the developer mentioned that clickable file links are preferred. Perhaps I could group the findings by file and summarize the results concisely, while ensuring no unintended changes were made during editing.
thinking
**Evaluating patch modifications**

I’m considering using `git diff -U0` to parse the edited lines. There are some existing unrelated modifications within the same file, which were already preexisting changes and shouldn’t be mentioned. It’s important to focus on our edits. I also need to keep in mind that line numbers could shift due to those preexisting modifications. Thankfully, I have line numbers available from `rg`. Should I also think about closing the agent? It might not be necessary, but I want to keep things clean.

@@ -741,14 +741,14 @@
 \end{proof}
 
 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}

 \begin{remark}[Connection and holonomy]\label{rem:propagator-vs-kernel}
-The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is a flat connection on the trivial line
-bundle over $C_2(X)$. The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is its holonomy: the
-parallel transport operator $\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$
-along a path from $(0,0)$ to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times
-\widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage from propagator to kernel is the
-passage from a Lie algebra element to its group element via the
-exponential map; the coalgebra structure of $\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$
-makes this exponential explicit at each bar degree.
+The propagator $d\log(z-w)$ is the Arnold/bar one-form on $C_2(X)$.
+The kernel $e^{x\xi}$ is obtained by exponentiating its path integral:
+$\exp\!\bigl(\int_\gamma d\log(z-w)\bigr)$ along a path from $(0,0)$
+to $(x,\xi)$ in $\mathbb{R} \times \widehat{\mathbb{R}}$. The passage
+from propagator to kernel is the passage from a Lie algebra element to
+its group element via the exponential map; the coalgebra structure of
+$\barB(\mathcal{H}_1)$ makes this exponential explicit at each bar
+degree.
 \end{remark}
 
 \begin{remark}[Four incarnations]\label{rem:four-transforms}
